# 04 — Add application-prod.yml Spring profile

Status: done
Labels: enhancement

*This was generated by AI during triage.*

## What

`application-dev.yml` exists but there is no `application-prod.yml`. All production config (DB URL, CORS, logging, WS origins) is either hardcoded in `application.yml` or missing entirely.

## Requirements

Create `src/main/resources/application-prod.yml` with:

```yaml
spring:
  datasource:
    url: ${DATABASE_URL}
    username: ${DATABASE_USERNAME}
    password: ${DATABASE_PASSWORD}

  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate   # already the base default, make it explicit

logging:
  level:
    root: WARN
    com.parchie: INFO

parchie:
  cors:
    allowed-origins: ${CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS}
```

- All sensitive values must be environment variables — no defaults for secrets in this file
- `DATABASE_URL` should be the full JDBC URL: `jdbc:postgresql://host:5432/parchie`
- WebSocket allowed origins: update `WebSocketConfig` to read `parchie.cors.allowed-origins` and use it in `setAllowedOriginPatterns` instead of `"*"` (currently line 20). Add that to this profile too.
- Do not include `spring.docker.compose` here (see `deployment/03`)

## Acceptance

Set environment variables and start with `SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=prod` — app connects to the provided DB, CORS is restricted to the provided origin, logs are at INFO/WARN levels.

## Files

- `src/main/resources/application-prod.yml` (new)
- `src/main/java/com/parchie/websocket/WebSocketConfig.java` — read allowed origins from config
- `src/main/java/com/parchie/config/AppConfig.java` — see also `deployment/02`
